perturbation.original_text: 1.3 MERCHANT CONTRACTS. NPC, as agent of NCB, shall be responsible for establishing the terms and conditions of the Merchant Contracts, including all changes thereunder, subject to Section 1.7 below. Without in any way limiting the authority granted in Section 1.1, NCB hereby authorizes NPC to use NCB's name to the extent necessary or appropriate in accordance with the terms of this Agreement to enter into and to renew Merchant Contracts, to modify Merchant Contracts to the extent necessary to assign them to NCB, to perform the Merchant Contracts and to take other necessary or appropriate actions with respect to the Merchant Contracts, all in accordance with requirements of Visa and MasterCard. As between NPC and NCB, NPC shall be responsible for all credit, fraud and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 MERCHANT CONTRACTS. NPC, as agent of NCB, shall be responsible for suggesting the terms and conditions of the Merchant Contracts, including all changes thereunder, subject to Section 1.7 below. While NPC can suggest changes, NCB maintains final authority over all contractual terms. Without in any way limiting the authority granted in Section 1.1, NCB hereby authorizes NPC to use NCB's name to the extent necessary or appropriate in accordance with the terms of this Agreement to enter into and to renew Merchant Contracts, to modify Merchant Contracts to the extent necessary to assign them to NCB, to perform the Merchant Contracts and to take other necessary or appropriate actions with respect to the Merchant Contracts, all in accordance with requirements of Visa and MasterCard. As between NPC and NCB, NPC shall be responsible for all credit, fraud and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces "shall be responsible for establishing" with "shall be responsible for suggesting," and adds that NCB maintains final authority. This introduces ambiguity regarding who ultimately controls the terms of the Merchant Contracts. While NPC 'suggests' terms, NCB's 'final authority' creates an In-Text contradiction to the responsibilities outlined later in Section 1.3, specifically which party is ultimately responsible for the terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 MERCHANT CONTRACTS. NPC, as agent of NCB, shall be responsible for suggesting the terms and conditions of the Merchant Contracts, including all changes thereunder, subject to Section 1.7 below. While NPC can suggest changes, NCB maintains final authority over all contractual terms. Without in any way limiting the authority granted in Section 1.1, NCB hereby authorizes NPC to use NCB's name to the extent necessary or appropriate in accordance with the terms of this Agreement to enter into and to renew Merchant Contracts, to modify Merchant Contracts to the extent necessary to assign them to NCB, to perform the Merchant Contracts and to take other necessary or appropriate actions with respect to the Merchant Contracts, all in accordance with requirements of Visa and MasterCard. As between NPC and NCB, NPC shall be responsible for all credit, fraud and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies NPC's role from 'establishing' terms to 'suggesting' them, while simultaneously granting NCB 'final authority'. This directly contradicts the subsequent statement in the same section that 'NPC shall be responsible for all credit, fraud and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract.' The contradiction lies in whether NPC, who bears the risks, also has the authority to define the contract terms. NCB's final authority creates ambiguity and inconsistency regarding ultimate responsibility, meeting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 1.7 DUE DILIGENCE BY NCB; NPC'S STANDARDS. (a) DUE DILIGENCE PRIOR TO EXECUTION OF AGREEMENT. Senior management officials of NPC have met with senior management officials of NCB and have discussed with the NCB officials the credit and financial review procedures and standards used by NPC in deciding whether to accept or retain Merchants as customers for Merchant Processing Services, as well as NPC's experience with respect to any losses resulting from financial failures or fraud by its merchant customers. Schedule A attached hereto outlines the credit and financial review procedures and standards currently used by NPC. NPC represents 2 3 to NCB, and has provided NCB's senior management with financial information showing, that for 1994 and 1995 combined, the losses incurred by NPC as a result of financial failures or fraud by its merchant customers have averaged less than .60% of NPC's net revenues from Merchant Processing Services.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.7 DUE DILIGENCE BY NCB; NPC'S STANDARDS. (a) DUE DILIGENCE PRIOR TO EXECUTION OF AGREEMENT. Senior management officials of NPC have met with senior management officials of NCB and have discussed with the NCB officials the credit and financial review procedures and standards used by NPC in deciding whether to accept or retain Merchants as customers for Merchant Processing Services. Schedule A attached hereto outlines some guidelines for credit and financial review, but NPC is not required to adhere strictly to these guidelines. NPC represents 2 3 to NCB, and has provided NCB's senior management with financial information showing, that for 1994 and 1995 combined, the losses incurred by NPC as a result of financial failures or fraud by its merchant customers have averaged less than .60% of NPC's net revenues from Merchant Processing Services.
perturbation.explanation: The change alters the meaning of Schedule A from outlining strict credit review procedures and standards currently used by NPC to 'some guidelines' that NPC isn't required to strictly adhere to. This creates an internal contradiction with Section 1.7(b), which implies that NPC will maintain consistent credit review procedures and standards and notify NCB of any material changes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.7 DUE DILIGENCE BY NCB; NPC'S STANDARDS. (b) CONTINUING DUE DILIGENCE: NPC'S RISK STANDARDS. NPC shall keep NCB's senior management advised of any material changes in the credit and financial review procedures and standards and of any material exceptions to such procedures and standards which may from time to time be made with respect to particular Merchants. NPC shall not make any such changes or exceptions until its management committee has approved such changes or exceptions after carefully evaluating the relative advantages and disadvantages expected to result from such changes or exceptions. NPC also shall continue to advise senior management officials of NCB on a timely basis with respect to any substantial loss (meaning any loss of $1,000,000 or more with respect to a single Merchant or $3,000,000 or more in the aggregate in any twelve month period) incurred by NPC as a result of any financial failures or fraud by its merchant customers. If NCB's senior management officials reasonably determine from time to time that any changes are needed in NPC's credit and financial review procedures and standards or in the implementation thereof in order to avoid any significant increase in NPC's losses from financial failures or fraud by its merchant customers, then NCB shall so notify NPC in writing, and NPC and NCB shall mutually agree upon, and NPC shall implement appropriate changes.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes Schedule A from strict procedures to mere guidelines that NPC isn't required to adhere to. This directly contradicts Section 1.7(b), which requires NPC to keep NCB informed of any material changes in its credit and financial review procedures and standards, and implies a consistent application of those procedures. By making the procedures non-binding, the change undermines the obligation in 1.7(b) and creates uncertainty about whether any changes need to be reported, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
